feedback.text: Sector-specific approach for flexible soft subscriptions in perishable goods requested. Feedback We welcome the Commissions work on the Digital Fairness Act to strengthen consumer protection online. As a European SME supplying physical, perishable pet food via flexible soft subscriptions (pause/skip/cancel anytime; no minimum term), we respectfully request a proportionate, sector-specific approach so that consumer rights are upheld without unintended harm to consumers, SMEs, or the environment. Why differentiation is needed The DFAs objectives address problems most acute in long-term, digital service contracts where lock-in and dark patterns can occur. In contrast, soft subscriptions for physical, short-cycle replenishment (e.g., pet food) already provide high consumer control (pause/skip/cancel) and carry logistics and perishability constraints that digital services do not. Treating these categories identically risks: Higher prices (operational inefficiency passed to consumers), Food waste (late cancellations after dispatch), and Lower access to convenient, reliable replenishment for essential goods. Our proposal (targeted exemptions/adjustments) Exempt flexible soft subscriptions for physical/perishable goods from repeated double opt-ins and pre-renewal mandatory reminders, provided there is: clear, prominent disclosure of auto-renewal and next-ship dates; an easy, self-serve way to pause/skip/cancel at any time; and no minimum term/lock-in. For the cancellation button obligation, allow a short operational buffer for perishable goods already packed or dispatched, so that orders in that state may be delivered (with clear upfront disclosure of the cut-off time). This prevents waste and preserves price stability while keeping cancellations immediate for all future cycles. In the legal text, distinguish between digital long-term contracts and physical short-cycle replenishment subscriptions. Define soft subscription (no minimum term; consumer can pause/skip/cancel anytime) and tailor obligations accordingly.
